steps.title: Identify the future time signal
steps.content: The strongest clue is “in a later chapter,” which indicates when the learning will occur—at a future point in the text.

steps.title: Match the tense to the timeframe
steps.content: The future tense “will learn” correctly expresses an action occurring at a future time, matching the “in a later chapter” reference. All other options—past perfect (had learned, had been learning) and present perfect (have learned)—place the action in the past or present, contradicting the future timeframe. That matches choice C — C is correct.
